rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dimethoxylation of olefins via anodic oxidation through a cation-initiated (ionic-like) pathway, with divergence to semipinacol-rearranged acetals for less-stabilized carbocations
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation; a second anodic oxidation of the β-methoxy carbon radical generates the key carbocation, while H2 is evolved at the cathode from MeOH reduction

key_intermediates.name: β-methoxy carbon-centered radical (i2) from MeOH addition to radical cation
β-methoxy carbocation (i3) after second anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the alkene (1) by SET to a radical cation i1, which is attacked by MeOH to give a β-methoxy C-radical i2; a second anodic SET gives carbocation i3, which is trapped by a second MeOH (path I, stabilized cations) or undergoes 1,2-aryl/alkyl migration to i4 before MeOH trapping (path II); protons reduced at cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Not the nucleophilic addition of MeOH (excluded by KIE studies); most likely the initial anodic oxidation of the alkene

probe_reagents.name: radical-clock substrate 1aa (cyclopropyl-substituted alkene)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded smoothly to 2aa without any ring-opening/ring-expansion products, arguing against a long-lived C-radical and supporting an ionic-like pathway

probe_reagents.name: deuterated MeOH (KIE experiment)
probe_reagents.observation: KIE indicated that nucleophilic addition of MeOH is not the rate-determining step

probe_reagents.name: MeOH / 1a / 1al cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: No oxidation peak for MeOH in the operating window; clear oxidation peaks for olefins 1a and 1al, showing alkene is oxidized preferentially and excluding methoxyl radical generation

notes: Two divergent pathways from the same cationic intermediate i3: path I direct MeOH trapping for stabilized (tri/diaryl) cations giving 1,2-dimethoxylation; path II semipinacol 1,2-migration for less stable (1,2-disubstituted) cations giving acetal products (Scheme 3). Authors explicitly exclude a methoxyl-radical pathway based on CV. A radical-cation intermediate is retained at the start, so a brief radical-polar crossover step is involved, but overall the productive C–O bond formation is described as ionic/cation-initiated.
